changelog: 1.0.0 — Lesson 005: 808 - Straight archetype for foundational low-end weight

mappings.designIntent: Establish a reliable, clean low-end foundation with sustained weight and clear transient articulation.
mappings.ampEnvelope: Uses a longer decay setting (or higher sustain) to ensure the sub energy carries through the note duration without premature fading.
mappings.harmonicSource: Likely driven by a pure Sine or near-sine wavetable with minimal harmonic saturation to prevent spectral scatter.
mappings.filterAndSpectral: Absence of high-pass filtering (Full Pass) to preserve the 40Hz-60Hz fundamental authority.
mappings.pitchEnvelope: Moderate pitch envelope depth and speed are used to create the soft transient 'knock' without becoming an aggressive click.
mappings.mixRole: Foundational bass voice; intended to carry the entire low-end weight of a track with high clarity and minimal harmonic clutter.

character: Full-range, clean 808 sub — smooth sine-like body with a defined but not overly aggressive transient; carries consistent low-end weight without midrange distortion or thinning.
causalReasoning: The sound is driven by a stable sine (or near-sine) oscillator with minimal harmonic saturation and no high-pass filtering. A moderate pitch envelope adds a soft transient “knock,” while a longer decay preserves sustained sub energy. The absence of distortion keeps the spectrum focused in the low frequencies, maintaining clarity and weight.
coreRules: Preserve sub continuity — the defining trait is sustained low-frequency energy, not transient character.
Avoid introducing distortion or aggressive filtering that would shift energy into mids and reduce perceived weight.

contrastWith.difference: Unlike Gucci’s transient-only, high-passed knock, Straight retains full sub-body and sustain. Gucci functions as a top/percussive layer, while Straight is a foundational bass voice carrying the low-end weight.
